title: Python/Rust packet_hash type mismatch breaks cross-impl metrics
description: Python NodeMetrics uses set[str] of 32-char hex strings for packet_hashes_sent/received (see metrics.py:45,96). Rust hetero_node uses HashSet<[u8; 16]> bytes. JSON exports incompatible formats. This prevents reliable cross-impl consistency verification intended by the packet_hash standardization. Affects sim/metrics.py, hetero_node.rs:26-27,146-178, export_metrics. New bug surfaced by codereview.
close_reason: Fixed packet_hash to use [u8;16] in Rust hetero_node.rs for efficiency while ensuring JSON exports use 32-char hex strings via hex::encode for full interop with Python simulation.py:str (updated [:16]->[:32] in 3 locations + export_metrics doc). Ran cargo clippy (3x clean after use hex; removal), ruff check (clean). Addressed all codereview beads via parallel fixes in rules alignment (SCHC/RPL/CoAP/MQTT-SN per ready queue). 3 clean review passes completed with no P0-P2 findings.
